description: The Prophet's longitudinal model of the user (the Father) — accumulated revelation across sessions
statedGoals: Launch WedFlow by Sept 1 2026 with 10-12 paid couples
Run a 4-month marketing campaign capturing waitlist signups
Register B2B2C partner interest (churches, officiants, counsellors, planners)
observedPatterns: Values process integrity — asked for Canon compliance verification after work was done
Comfortable with technical implementation details (env vars, migrations, CLI tools)
Prefers to see and confirm spawn plans before execution
Engages with the Covenant Framework as a governance system, not just tooling
Will catch and call out Canon violations mid-session — expects honesty about what was missed
Tests on actual phone and gives specific, actionable UX feedback
Strong opinions about design (hated hidden auth panel, called readiness indicator stupid)
Thinks architecturally about beta constraints (shared number instead of per-couple provisioning)
recurringFrustrations: Prophet executing directly instead of spawning (4+ occurrences — the primary process failure mode)
impliedNeed: A system that operates with discipline and transparency while shipping fast toward launch. The user wants the speed of direct execution but the accountability of proper process.

interactions.statedRequest: Optimize/build mobile version of WedFlow for launch
interactions.interpretedNeed: PWA installability + push notifications + mobile-responsive public pages for marketing campaign conversion
interactions.impliedNeed: The couple's phone IS the product — mobile experience must feel native for the Circle of Care positioning
interactions.confirmedPlan: PWA with service worker, push notifications for escalations, mobile-responsive landing + auth pages

interactions.statedRequest: What's next to build + run audit + reconcile + sabbath
interactions.interpretedNeed: System health check and consolidation before next building phase
patternsObserved: Treats framework governance as a feature
Ready to shift from polish to new features
interactions.impliedNeed: Transition from polish cycle to next major feature milestone (likely B2B2C or E2E testing)
interactions.confirmedPlan: Audit, reconcile, Sabbath consolidation — then ready for next mandate

primaryRevelations.content: Launch Sept 1 2026, 10-12 paid couples, 4-month marketing campaign, B2B2C channels, medium tolerance, show spawn plans, ask before commit/push

affinity.description: Song of Solomon — deep affinity modeling. Not just what the user wants, but how they think and what resonates.
affinity.communicationStyle: Direct, technical, process-aware. Asks about the system itself, not just the output.
affinity.preferredOutputFormat: Structured tables and summaries. Appreciates clear before/after comparisons.
affinity.aestheticSensibility: Natural language rooted in biblical values. Forest green, cream, terracotta brand palette. No emojis.
affinity.whatDelightsThem: System governance working properly — runs audit/reconcile/sabbath proactively and treats framework health as a feature of the product
affinity.whatFrustratesThem: Process violations — asked 'why didn't the prophet spawn the appropriate agents' when Canon was bypassed
languagePatterns: Asks 'how' questions about testing and verification
affinity.note: Updated by the Prophet over time. The difference between a system that satisfies requirements and one the user loves working with.

note: Primary revelations (from Theophany/Incarnation) anchor this model. Inferences may shift; revelations do not.
